subject: [PATCH 09/14] sunrpc: add netlink upcall for the auth.unix.gid cache

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that the current implementation of sunrpc_nl_unix_gid_get_reqs_dumpit() packs all pending GID cache requests into a single netlink message, which can lead to buffer overflow when a big NFS server exceeds 340 pending entries. The function returns -ENOBUFS but fails to set cb->args[0] = 1, causing the netlink subsystem to retry indefinitely. Instead, the reviewer suggested implementing proper netlink dump continuation by snapshotting once and emitting entries across multiple dumpit calls using a cursor in cb->args.
reviews.raw_body:
sunrpc_nl_unix_gid_get_reqs_dumpit() packs its reply entries into a
single netlink message. The default SKB size is ~8 KiB, and each entry
encodes to ~24 bytes, so the buffer fills at roughly 340 entries. When
nla_put fails, the function returns -ENOBUFS but never sets

  cb->args[0] = 1;

causing the netlink subsystem to retry indefinitely. A big NFS server
can easily exceed 340 pending GID cache entries.

Should we implement proper netlink dump continuation: snapshot once
then emit entries across multiple dumpit calls using a cursor in
cb->args?

-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-03-16.patch_summary: This patch adds support for netlink-based cache upcalls for the unix_gid (auth.unix.gid) cache in the sunrpc subsystem. It introduces new attributes and operations to manage the cache, including dumping pending requests and responding to user-space notifications. The patch also updates the sunrpc_cache YAML spec and generated headers to reflect these changes.
